{
  "name": "firelab-platform",
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "prisma generate && next build --no-lint",
    "start": "next start --hostname 0.0.0.0 --port 3000",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "lint": "next lint",

    "prisma:migrate": "prisma migrate dev",
    "prisma:deploy": "node scripts/check-required-env.cjs DATABASE_URL && node scripts/ensure-prisma-migration.cjs && node scripts/recover-prisma-migration.cjs && node scripts/resolve-prisma-migration.cjs && prisma migrate deploy",
    "prisma:push": "prisma db push",
    "prisma:studio": "prisma studio",
    "seed": "tsx prisma/seed.ts"
  },
  "prisma": {
    "seed": "tsx prisma/seed.ts"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/client": "^5.20.0",
    "bcryptjs": "^2.4.3",
    "class-variance-authority": "^0.7.0",
    "clsx": "^2.1.1",
    "framer-motion": "^11.11.0",

    "jsonwebtoken": "^9.0.2",
    "lucide-react": "^0.451.0",
    "next": "^15.1.0",
    "react": "^19.0.0",

  },
  "devDependencies": {

    "@types/node": "^20.14.0",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.0",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "@types/uuid": "^10.0.0",
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.19",
    "postcss": "^8.4.38",
    "prisma": "^5.20.0",
    "tailwindcss": "^3.4.4",
    "tsx": "^4.19.2",
    "typescript": "5.8.2"
  }
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

enum Role {
  ADMIN
  SELLER
  DEVELOPER
  USER
}

enum TeamRole {
  OWNER
  MANAGER
  MEMBER
}

enum ScriptVisibility {
  PUBLIC
  PRIVATE
  OBFUSCATED
}

enum ScriptStatus {
  ACTIVE
  MAINTENANCE
  DISABLED
}

enum LicenseType {
  LIFETIME
  SUBSCRIPTION
}

enum LicenseStatus {
  UNREDEEMED
  ACTIVE
  SUSPENDED
  EXPIRED
  BLACKLISTED
}

model User {
  id                     String            @id @default(uuid())
  username               String            @unique
  displayName            String?
  email                  String            @unique

  passwordHash           String
  role                   Role              @default(USER)

  discordId              String?           @unique
  githubId               String?           @unique
  avatarUrl              String?
  emailVerifiedAt        DateTime?
  isVerified             Boolean           @default(false)

  twoFactorSecretEncrypted String?
  twoFactorEnabled       Boolean           @default(false)

  banned                 Boolean           @default(false)
  banReason              String?
  bannedUntil            DateTime?

  createdAt              DateTime          @default(now())
  updatedAt              DateTime          @updatedAt

  sessions               Session[]
  emailVerificationTokens EmailVerificationToken[]
  passwordResetTokens     PasswordResetToken[]
  oAuthStates            OAuthState[]
  linkedAccounts         LinkedAccount[]
  recoveryCodes          RecoveryCode[]
  emailChangeTokens      EmailChangeToken[]
  loginHistory           LoginAttempt[]
  devices                UserDevice[]
  notifications          Notification[]
  savedSearches          SavedSearch[]
  scriptCollaborations   ScriptCollaborator[]
  scripts                Script[]          @relation("OwnedScripts")
  createdScriptVersions  ScriptVersion[]   @relation("CreatedScriptVersions")
  authoredAnnouncements  Announcement[]    @relation("AuthoredAnnouncements")
  licenses               License[]         @relation("OwnedLicenses")
  sales                  Sale[]
  auditLogs              AuditLog[]
  apiTokens              ApiToken[]
  webhooks               Webhook[]
  teamMemberships        TeamMember[]
  teamInvitesCreated     TeamInvite[]
  ownedTeams             Team[]            @relation("TeamOwner")
  customDomains          CustomDomain[]
  staff                  Staff?
  managedStaff           Staff[]         @relation("StaffManagerUser")
  inboundWebhookSecrets  InboundWebhookSecret[]
  monetizationLinks     MonetizationLink[]
}

model Session {
  id             String   @id @default(uuid())
  sessionToken   String   @unique
  userId         String
  user           User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  userAgent      String?
  ip             String?
  deviceName     String?
  fingerprintHash String?
  trusted        Boolean  @default(false)
  revokedAt      DateTime?
  expiresAt      DateTime
  lastUsedAt     DateTime?
  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([userId])
  @@index([expiresAt])
  @@index([revokedAt])
}

model EmailVerificationToken {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  tokenHash String   @unique
  expiresAt DateTime
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([userId])
}

model PasswordResetToken {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  tokenHash String   @unique
  expiresAt DateTime
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([userId])
}

model OAuthState {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String?
  user      User?    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  state                  String   @unique
  codeVerifierEncrypted String?
  createdAt            DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([userId])
}

model Team {
  id        String        @id @default(uuid())
  name      String
  ownerId   String
  owner     User          @relation("TeamOwner", fields: [ownerId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  createdAt DateTime      @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime      @updatedAt

  members   TeamMember[]
  invites   TeamInvite[]
  scripts   Script[]
  domains   CustomDomain[]
}

model TeamMember {
  id        String    @id @default(uuid())
  teamId    String
  team      Team      @relation(fields: [teamId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  userId    String
  user      User      @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  role      TeamRole  @default(MEMBER)
  permissions Json?
  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())

  @@unique([teamId, userId])
}

model TeamInvite {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  teamId      String
  team        Team     @relation(fields: [teamId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  email       String
  role        TeamRole @default(MEMBER)
  permissions Json?
  tokenHash   String   @unique
  createdBy   String
  creator     User     @relation(fields: [createdBy], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  expiresAt   DateTime
  usedAt      DateTime?
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([teamId, usedAt])
  @@index([expiresAt])
}

model CustomDomain {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  domain      String   @unique
  userId      String?
  user        User?    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  teamId      String?
  team        Team?    @relation(fields: [teamId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  dnsChallenge String  @unique
  verifiedAt  DateTime?
  primary     Boolean  @default(false)
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([userId])
  @@index([teamId])
}

model Script {
  id              String           @id @default(uuid())
  publicId        String           @unique @default(cuid())
  name            String
  description     String?
  version         String           @default("1.0.0")
  contentEncrypted String          @db.Text
  contentHash     String
  visibility      ScriptVisibility @default(PRIVATE)
  status          ScriptStatus     @default(ACTIVE)
  category        String?
  iconUrl         String?
  getKeyLink      String?
  keyMode         String?          @default("normal")
  channel         ScriptChannel    @default(STABLE)
  protectionProfileId String?
  protectionProfile ScriptProtectionProfile? @relation(fields: [protectionProfileId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  ownerId         String
  owner           User             @relation("OwnedScripts", fields: [ownerId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  teamId          String?
  team            Team?            @relation(fields: [teamId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  createdAt       DateTime         @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime         @updatedAt

  licenses        License[]
  versions        ScriptVersion[]
  collaborators   ScriptCollaborator[]
  releases        ScriptRelease[]
  downloads       ScriptDownload[]
  accessLogs      ScriptAccessLog[]
  monetizationLinks MonetizationLink[]

  @@index([ownerId, status])
  @@index([category, status])
}

model ScriptVersion {
  id               String   @id @default(uuid())
  scriptId         String
  script           Script   @relation(fields: [scriptId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  version          String
  channel          ScriptChannel @default(STABLE)
  contentEncrypted String   @db.Text
  contentHash      String
  changeLog        String?
  releaseNotes     String?
  isRollback       Boolean @default(false)
  createdBy        String
  creator          User     @relation("CreatedScriptVersions", fields: [createdBy], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  createdAt        DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([scriptId, createdAt])
}

model License {
  id               String        @id @default(uuid())
  keyEncrypted     String        @db.Text
  keyHash          String        @unique
  type             LicenseType   @default(LIFETIME)
  status           LicenseStatus @default(UNREDEEMED)
  durationDays     Int?
  expiresAt        DateTime?
  redeemedAt       DateTime?
  redeemedBy       String?
  note             String?
  hwidLock         Boolean       @default(true)
  hwidResetCount   Int           @default(0)
  hwidResetLimit   Int           @default(3)
  blacklistReason  String?
  groupId          String?
  group            LicenseGroup? @relation(fields: [groupId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  tags             Json?
  trial            Boolean       @default(false)
  productId        String?
  transferable     Boolean       @default(true)
  scriptId         String
  script           Script        @relation(fields: [scriptId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  ownerId          String
  owner            User          @relation("OwnedLicenses", fields: [ownerId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  createdAt        DateTime      @default(now())
  updatedAt        DateTime      @updatedAt

  hwids            Hwid[]
  activations      Activation[]
  sales            Sale[]

  @@index([ownerId, status])
  @@index([scriptId, status])
  @@index([expiresAt])
}

model Hwid {
  id             String    @id @default(uuid())
  licenseId      String
  license        License   @relation(fields: [licenseId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  fingerprintHash String
  source         String?
  lastIp         String?
  lastDeviceInfo String?
  activations    Activation[]
  createdAt      DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt      DateTime  @updatedAt

  @@unique([licenseId, fingerprintHash])
  @@index([fingerprintHash])
}

model Activation {
  id              String    @id @default(uuid())
  licenseId       String
  license         License   @relation(fields: [licenseId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  hwidId          String?
  hwid            Hwid?     @relation(fields: [hwidId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  fingerprintHash String
  ip              String
  country         String?
  deviceInfo      String?
  userAgent       String?
  success         Boolean   @default(true)
  failureReason   String?
  createdAt       DateTime  @default(now())

  @@index([licenseId, createdAt])
  @@index([fingerprintHash, createdAt])
}

model Sale {
  id                   String   @id @default(uuid())
  sellerId             String
  seller               User     @relation(fields: [sellerId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  licenseId            String?
  license              License? @relation(fields: [licenseId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  licenseKeyHash       String
  amount               Decimal  @db.Decimal(12, 2)
  currency             String   @default("USD")
  gateway              String
  gatewayTransactionId String?
  commissionRate       Decimal  @default(2.5) @db.Decimal(5, 2)
  commissionAmount     Decimal  @db.Decimal(12, 2)
  createdAt            DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([sellerId, createdAt])
  @@index([licenseKeyHash])
}

model AuditLog {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String?
  user      User?    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  action    String
  targetType String?
  targetId  String?
  details   Json?
  ip        String?
  userAgent String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([userId, createdAt])
  @@index([action, createdAt])
}

model ApiToken {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  name      String
  tokenHash String   @unique
  prefix    String
  expiresAt DateTime?
  lastUsedAt DateTime?
  permissions Json?
  active Boolean @default(true)
  blacklisted Boolean @default(false)
  blacklistReason String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([userId])
}

model Webhook {
  id             String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId         String
  user           User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  url            String
  events         Json
  secretEncrypted String?
  active         Boolean  @default(true)
  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())
  deliveries     WebhookDelivery[]

  @@index([userId, active])
}

model InboundWebhookSecret {
  id              String   @id @default(uuid())
  provider        String   @unique
  secretEncrypted String
  active          Boolean  @default(true)
  allowedEvents   Json?
  userId          String?
  user            User?    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt

  @@index([active])
}

model Announcement {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  title     String
  content   String   @db.Text
  authorId  String
  author    User     @relation("AuthoredAnnouncements", fields: [authorId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  active    Boolean  @default(true)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  @@index([active, createdAt])
}

model SiteSetting {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  key       String   @unique
  value     Json
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
}

model Staff {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String   @unique
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  role      String
  scopes    Json
  active    Boolean  @default(true)
  managedBy String?
  manager   User?    @relation("StaffManagerUser", fields: [managedBy], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  @@index([active])
}

model LinkedAccount {
  id                  String        @id @default(uuid())
  userId              String
  user                User          @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  provider            OAuthProvider
  providerAccountId   String
  accessTokenEncrypted String?
  refreshTokenEncrypted String?
  expiresAt           DateTime?
  scopes              Json?
  linkedAt            DateTime      @default(now())
  lastSyncedAt        DateTime?

  @@unique([userId, provider, providerAccountId])
  @@index([provider, providerAccountId])
  @@index([expiresAt])
}

model RecoveryCode {
  id          String    @id @default(uuid())
  userId      String
  user        User      @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  codeHash    String    @unique
  consumedAt  DateTime?
  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now())

  @@index([userId])
}

model EmailChangeToken {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  email     String
  tokenHash String   @unique
  expiresAt DateTime
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([userId])
}

model LoginAttempt {
  id             String    @id @default(uuid())
  userId         String?
  user           User?     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  email          String?
  success        Boolean   @default(false)
  reason         String?
  ip             String?
  userAgent      String?
  deviceName     String?
  fingerprintHash String?
  createdAt      DateTime  @default(now())

  @@index([userId, createdAt])
  @@index([ip, createdAt])
  @@index([fingerprintHash, createdAt])
}

model UserDevice {
  id              String    @id @default(uuid())
  userId          String
  user            User      @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  fingerprintHash String
  deviceName      String?
  lastIp          String?
  lastDeviceInfo  String?
  lastUserAgent   String?
  trusted         Boolean   @default(false)
  blocked         Boolean   @default(false)
  lastSeenAt      DateTime  @default(now())
  createdAt       DateTime  @default(now())

  @@unique([userId, fingerprintHash])
  @@index([fingerprintHash])
}

model LicenseGroup {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  ownerId     String
  name        String
  description String?
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
  licenses    License[]

  @@unique([ownerId, name])
  @@index([ownerId])
}

model LicenseTransferRequest {
  id              String        @id @default(uuid())
  licenseId       String
  fromUserId      String
  toUserEmail     String
  status          TransferStatus @default(PENDING)
  note            String?
  expiresAt       DateTime
  createdAt       DateTime      @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime      @updatedAt

  @@index([fromUserId, status])
  @@index([licenseId, status])
}

model LicenseRestorationRequest {
  id              String   @id @default(uuid())
  licenseId       String
  userId          String
  reason          String
  status          ResetRequestStatus @default(PENDING)
  reviewedBy      String?
  reviewNote      String?
  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt

  @@index([userId, status])
  @@index([licenseId])
}

model HwidResetRequest {
  id              String   @id @default(uuid())
  licenseId       String
  userId          String
  reason          String
  status          ResetRequestStatus @default(PENDING)
  reviewedBy      String?
  reviewNote      String?
  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt

  @@index([userId, status])
  @@index([licenseId])
}

model ScriptProtectionProfile {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  ownerId     String
  name        String
  hwidLock    Boolean  @default(true)
  signatureRequired Boolean @default(true)
  maxActivations Int?
  allowDebuggerDetection Boolean @default(true)
  scripts     Script[]

  @@unique([ownerId, name])
  @@index([ownerId])
}

model ScriptCollaborator {
  id          String    @id @default(uuid())
  scriptId    String
  script      Script    @relation(fields: [scriptId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  userId      String
  user        User      @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now())

  @@unique([scriptId, userId])
  @@index([userId])
}

model ScriptRelease {
  id              String   @id @default(uuid())
  scriptId        String
  script          Script   @relation(fields: [scriptId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  versionId       String
  channel         ScriptChannel @default(STABLE)
  notes           String?
  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([scriptId, channel])
}

model ScriptDownload {
  id              String    @id @default(uuid())
  scriptId        String
  script          Script    @relation(fields: [scriptId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  userId          String?
  ip              String?
  userAgent       String?
  createdAt       DateTime  @default(now())

  @@index([scriptId, createdAt])
}

model ScriptAccessLog {
  id              String    @id @default(uuid())
  scriptId        String
  script          Script    @relation(fields: [scriptId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  licenseId       String?
  userId          String?
  ip              String?
  country         String?
  success         Boolean   @default(false)
  reason          String?
  createdAt       DateTime  @default(now())

  @@index([scriptId, createdAt])
  @@index([licenseId, createdAt])
}

model Notification {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  type      NotificationType
  status    NotificationStatus @default(UNREAD)
  title     String
  message   String   @db.Text
  actionUrl String?
  metadata  Json?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  readAt    DateTime?

  @@index([userId, status])
  @@index([userId, createdAt])
}

model SavedSearch {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  scope     String
  name      String
  filters   Json
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  @@unique([userId, scope, name])
  @@index([userId, scope])
}

model UserReport {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  reporterId  String?
  targetUserId String?
  targetType  String
  targetId    String
  reason      String
  details     String?
  status      ReportStatus @default(OPEN)
  reviewedBy  String?
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt

  @@index([status, createdAt])
  @@index([targetType, targetId])
}

model SellerVerification {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId      String
  status      ReportStatus @default(OPEN)
  details     Json?
  reviewedBy  String?
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt

  @@index([status, createdAt])
}

model WebhookDelivery {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  webhookId   String
  webhook     Webhook  @relation(fields: [webhookId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  event       String
  status      DeliveryStatus @default(PENDING)
  attempt     Int      @default(0)
  responseStatus Int?
  responseBody String?
  nextRetryAt DateTime?

  @@index([webhookId, createdAt])
  @@index([status, nextRetryAt])
}

model IpReputation {
  id              String   @id @default(uuid())
  ip              String   @unique
  score           Int      @default(100)
  country         String?
  asn             String?
  vpnProxy        Boolean  @default(false)
  suspiciousLoginCount Int @default(0)
  blocked         Boolean  @default(false)
  lastSeenAt      DateTime @default(now())
  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([score])
}

model SecretRotation {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  secretName  String
  status      SecretRotationStatus @default(PENDING)
  previousHash String
  newHash     String
  rotatedBy   String?
  note        String?
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  completedAt DateTime?

  @@index([secretName, status])
}

model ApiRequestMetric {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  route       String
  method      String
  statusCode  Int
  latencyMs   Int

  @@index([statusCode, createdAt])
}

model ErrorEvent {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())

  @@index([name, createdAt])
}

model EmailNotification {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId      String?
  to          String
  subject     String

}

This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror search content tools. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 100). Use to limit output for broad patterns. When the cap is hit the result is paginated; pass 'offset' set to the previous offset+maxResults to continue.

offset.description: Optional: Number of matching file paths to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text files. For images, PDFs, videos, audio or other multimedia files, this tool cannot present their contents to the model — use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool instead.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once. Note: search_file_content reports matches as 1-based `Lxx` line numbers; to jump from a hit at `L42` here, subtract 1 — pass `offset: 41` (i.e. `offset = lineNumber - 1`).

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. The search backend is ripgrep, so regex patterns use ripgrep/Rust regex syntax, not JavaScript RegExp syntax. If you need to search literal text that contains regex metacharacters (for example $, {, [, (, or \), set fixed_strings: true or -F: true instead of escaping by hand. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. By default, returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers; set output_mode to "files_with_matches" for just file paths or "count" for per-file match totals. Each matched line is truncated at 500 characters with a "[line truncated from N chars]" suffix to keep minified or base64 content from blowing up context.

pattern.description: The regular expression pattern to search for within file contents. This is interpreted by ripgrep/Rust regex, not JavaScript RegExp; avoid JS-only features such as lookbehind and backreferences. Regex syntax errors are returned by ripgrep. For literal text, especially strings containing regex metacharacters like $, {, [, (, or \, prefer fixed_strings: true or -F: true. When passing JSON args, escape only for JSON once: to match literal ${, use pattern "\\$\\{" (the regex engine sees \$\{), not "\\\\$\\\\{".

path.description: Optional: A path relative to the workspace root, or an absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

glob.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Single string only — combine multiple patterns via whitespace/comma separation (e.g. '*.js *.ts', '*.js,*.ts') or brace expansion ('*.{ts,tsx}'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

output_mode.description: Optional: Output format. 'content' (default) returns matching lines with line numbers and optional context; 'files_with_matches' returns only the list of matching file paths; 'count' returns per-file match counts.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 250, max: 1000). Values above 1000 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches. Pass 0 for unlimited.

head_limit.description: Optional: Claude Code aligned alias for 'maxResults'. When both are provided, head_limit wins. Pass 0 for unlimited (use sparingly — large result sets waste context).

offset.description: Optional: Number of matches to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

-A.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -A.

-B.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -B.

-C.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -C.

context.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for -C.

-n.description: Optional: Whether to include line numbers in 'content' output. Defaults to true; set to false to omit them.

-i.description: Optional: Case-insensitive matching. Defaults to false (case-sensitive), matching ripgrep behavior.

fixed_strings.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for -F.

-F.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for fixed_strings.

type.description: Optional: ripgrep file type filter (rg --type). Common types: js, py, rust, go, java, etc. Accepts any alias from `rg --type-list`; the value is forwarded verbatim to ripgrep.

multiline.description: Optional: Enable multiline matching where patterns may span newlines (passes -U and --multiline-dotall to ripgrep). Only meaningful for 'content' output_mode. ^/$ anchors keep line boundaries behavior for portability across search backends.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror ripgrep's default hidden-skip behaviour. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly (passes --hidden to ripgrep).
